description: In this keynote, the speaker discusses the concept of a 'computer in the sky,' a global neutral infrastructure for general purpose computation, emphasizing its potential as a public good and the challenges in realizing such technology. The talk reflects on the evolution of communication technologies and draws parallels to the envisioned computing infrastructure, highlighting the importance of blockchain protocols and secure digital ownership.
topic_summary: The keynote explores the idea of a decentralized, robust computing infrastructure akin to the Internet, focusing on its implications for digital ownership and the role of blockchain technology in achieving this vision. The speaker also pays tribute to Luca Trebison and shares insights from his academic and professional journey.
